extern type Number;
extern type Bool;
cap Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_sub(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
extern fn js_is_zero(n: Number): Bool;
impl Number: Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_sub(a, b)) }

fn is_even(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 1 } else { is_odd(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn is_odd(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 0 } else { is_even(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn main(): Number = is_even(4)

===EXPECT===
# Mutual recursion: `is_even` calls `is_odd` and vice versa. v1 limitation:
# dep_free's fixed-point iteration can't break this cycle because neither fn
# is ever "known dep-free" — each requires the other to be classified first.
# After fixed-point stabilizes, both remain `Unknown` and are NOT optimized.
#
# Fixture asserts the negative: no clone for either fn, and both keep their
# CPS form with `__caps.Sub_Number.sub(...)`. `__main_cps` calls the CPS
# `is_even` directly.
#
# Fixing mutual recursion would require SCC-level analysis (classify a whole
# cycle dep-free if all its external dependencies are). Out of scope for v1.
function is_even(__caps, n, __k)
function is_odd(__caps, n, __k)
__caps.Sub_Number.sub
is_even(__caps, __cps_v_1, __k)
is_odd(__caps, __cps_v_0, __k)
!is_even__lto
!is_odd__lto
